channel.title: Jira
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LS-94] 7.8 Generation History (n8_generation_history)
item.description:
Epic: LS-84 To Do

Goal: Maintain an auditable, trackable history of all audio generations per POI. Provide Creators with a clear UI to list, review, download, and manage which specific version of the generated audio is set as the "Active" track to be carried forward to the final audio guide.

Requirements:

  1. POI-Level History List UI:
    • The UI must feature a "Generation History" panel for each POI/item.
    • This panel displays a chronological list of every audio track generated for that specific spot, separated by language.
  2. Review & Download Capabilities:
    • Preview: Creators must be able to hit "Play" directly within the history list to listen to and evaluate any previous audio iteration.
    • Download: Each iteration must have a visible "Download" button allowing the Creator to save the specific .mp3 file.
  3. Default Active State & Carry-Forward Logic (CRITICAL):
    • Default Selection: By default, the system automatically marks the most recently generated audio track as the "Active" version.
    • Manual Override: Creators can securely click a "Set as Active" button on any older version in the list. This dynamically re-binds that older .mp3 run as the "Active" track.
    • Pipeline Carry-Forward: The system strictly carries forward only the version currently marked as "Active" to the next step (Stage 4: Preview & Publish) and the final Laxy Player.
  4. Audit Logging & Metadata Display:
    • Each historical entry in the UI must display key context: Timestamp, requested Language, and the Character/Voice setup used.
    • Admin Traceability: Log exact Token usage estimates and generation parameters for every single run to support cost analysis.
  5. Asset Tracking:
    • Track and preserve the physical Firebase Storage .mp3 object links dynamically tied to each historical iteration (do not overwrite the actual files in Cloud Storage when a new generation occurs).

Acceptance Criteria:

  • [ ] The UI successfully renders a chronological list of audio generations for each individual POI/item.
  • [ ] The most recently generated audio is automatically assigned the "Active" status by default.
  • [ ] Creators can manually select and change the "Active" status to any older version in the history list.
  • [ ] Only the .mp3 file associated with the "Active" version is carried forward to the Preview & Publish stage and the final player payload.
  • [ ] Creators can click to play/preview or download any .mp3 audio version directly from the history list.
  • [ ] The database natively stores an array/collection of historical generation runs per spot, securely linking the Firebase Storage URL to its Timestamp.
  • [ ] The system accurately logs the Token cost estimation, character metadata, and languages queried per historical entry.
